Globba tetrandra, commonly known as the Four-angled Globba, is a plant species with a unique, four-angled stem. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Globba tetrandra include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Globba tetrandra include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
